primary_accounting_issue: Définition IFRS du critère « principal et intérêts uniquement » (SPPI) pour la classification des actifs financiers

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS and selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the classification question and defines when contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty, because IFRS 9 directly addresses the SPPI criterion and resulting classification models, while IAS 32 only provides background definitions of financial instruments.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define the SPPI criterion and the classification outcomes for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs provide the foundational definition of a financial asset and the meaning of contractual rights, which support the SPPI analysis.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs discuss financial assets in service concession arrangements, but not the SPPI definition or peer classification approaches.
These paragraphs concern employee benefit assets and reimbursement rights, not SPPI-based classification of financial assets.
These paragraphs address contingent consideration classification and subsequent measurement, not the SPPI criterion for financial assets.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held in a hold-to-collect business model

Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held in a business model achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets
Financial assets that fail the SPPI criterion or are not held in a qualifying business model

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 requires this measurement basis when the asset meets the SPPI criterion and is held in a business model to collect contractual cash flows.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 requires this measurement basis when the asset meets the SPPI criterion and is held in a business model achieved by both collecting cash flows and selling assets.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 requires this measurement basis when the asset does not satisfy the SPPI criterion or otherwise does not qualify for the other two measurement categories.
